context.pressure: Your previous decision is now shaping how the team interprets leadership.
context.narrative: A few days pass. The clinic is quieter, but something has shifted. Conversations feel shorter. You overhear a comment: 'I guess if it gets hard enough, you can just step down.' Some team members seem distant, others are watching you more carefully. Your previous decision is now becoming a story the team is learning from.

choices.label: Ignore the shift in tone and focus on keeping the clinic running smoothly.
Address the team briefly, explaining the reasoning behind your decision and reaffirming expectations.
Speak directly with team members who seem affected and ask what they believe your decision meant.

questions.level_2: What in this team is still worth protecting, even now?
questions.level_3: What did your previous choice teach the team about what is acceptable?
questions.level_4: How is your behavior now shaping what others believe leadership allows?
